summary: Fix export warning remaining visible after deleting last export preset

description.text:
<!--
Please target the `master` branch in priority.

Relevant fixes are cherry-picked for stable branches as needed by maintainers.

To speed up the contribution process and avoid CI errors, please set up pre-commit hooks locally:
https://contributing.godotengine.org/en/latest/engine/guidelines/code_style.html
-->

Summary:
When deleting the last export preset in the Project Export dialog, the warning bar at the bottom remains visible even though no preset is selected.

What the Cause is:
It appears that the __edit_preset() function hides all preset dependent error labels when there is no valid preset index, but does not hid export_warning. This would explain why the previous warning text remains visible.

Fix:
The fix is to hide export_warning when p_index is invalid.

For testing steps, create an export preset that triggers a warning. Delete the preset, and the warning bar should now correctly disappear.

This fixes #113428

summary: Adding classes through add node is not always good

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in any Godot 4 version

### System information

Any

### Issue description

One of the greatest features that was added in Godot 4 is that when you set a `class_name` on your script, Godot now recognises that class as a proper class and you can add a node for that class directly from the add node dialog.

Seriously, greatest thing since sliced bread. However there is one weakness here.

In XR Tools we have many implementations where a scene + script is needed, but where we still set a class name on the script so we can reference nodes of that type with typing enabled. When adding a node through the `add child node` dialog, only the script is used, not the scene, and it breaks.

I get bug reports from users with some regularity that XR tools is broken, because they used the `add child node` option instead of the `Instantiate Child Scene` option. Even veteran users run into this especially now that we have a number of nodes that don't use scenes which leads to further confusion.

It would be really nice if either a script can be marked as not addable through the `add child node` or better yet, if we can identify the scene that should be instanced.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Create a new Node3D scene
2. Add a Label3D node to this scene
3. Add a script on the root node:

```
class_name MyTestNode
extends Node3D

func _ready():
  $Label3D.text = "hello world"
```

4. Create another scene, dress it up with a camera and world environment etc.
5. Add `MyTestNode` using the `Add Child Node` button
6. Run program.


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-node-issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23944510/test-node-issue.zip)

summary: Fix TileMap Dock button and warning label placement

description.text: This PR fixes two bugs; the first is that clicking on a TileMap node (or any node without a TileSet) while in horizontal orientation would show the warning label in the wrong place: The second bug is that while on a TileMap in vertical orientation, there was an extra wide control that is visible, increasing the size of the side bar unnecessarily. This PR moves that control to the top where all the other wide controls are. It also improves the horizontal experience by having the wide controls be in the same place as before #113128 occurred.

summary: No way to release captured mouse in editor while running project on macOS(?)

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.6.dev.custom_build [63e14e13f]

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (63e14e13f) - macOS Sequoia (15.7.2) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Pro - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads) - 24.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

When running a project where the mouse is locked with
```gdscript
Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED
```
there is not, as far as I can tell, any way to force-release the mouse in order to interact with the Godot editor. The developer must program into their project an explicit check to release the mouse if a button is pressed. Thus, the only way to stop running the project is to use the Command + Period shortcut or use Command + Q, which attempts to shut down the whole editor.

It sounds like this may be a macOS-specific issue; another user in RocketChat said that using Wayland on Linux, they were able to force the mouse's release by using F8, the Super key, and Alt + Tab, but none of these approaches worked for me on macOS. 

### Steps to reproduce

Run the attached project, or create a new project where the root node has a script that runs
```gdscript
Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED
```
Make sure the project runs embedded and not floating in the editor. Once it starts, there will be no way to unlock the cursor other than using the Command + Period or Command + Q.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test-mouse-lock_2025-12-04_12-43-49.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23943652/test-mouse-lock_2025-12-04_12-43-49.zip)

summary: Mouse not released when quitting embedded, non-floating game in editor

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in v4.6.dev.custom_build [63e14e13f]

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (63e14e13f) - macOS Sequoia (15.7.2) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - Apple M4 Pro - Apple M4 Pro (14 threads) - 24.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

If the mouse cursor is locked by a project using
```gdscript
Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED
```
when the project is closed from the editor using the shortcut (Cmd + Period on macOS), the cursor remains locked and the editor becomes unusable.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8acfbaf5-7003-45d0-8c4f-12e4f5539fe9

### Steps to reproduce

Create a new project. In the project, add a simple script like this:
```gdscript
extends Control

func _ready():
    Input.mouse_mode = Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED
```

Under the Game tab's Embedding options:
- Check "Embed Game on Next Play"
- Uncheck "Make Game Workspace Floating on Next Play"

Run the scene. The mouse cursor should disappear as it is captured.

Stop the project, such as by using the keyboard shortcut (Cmd + Period on macOS). The game view will disappear as if the project is stopped, but the mouse will not reappear, and click events will remain at the same point on the screen.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

The attached project should be set up and ready to run: 

[test-mouse-lock_2025-12-04_12-43-49.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23943419/test-mouse-lock_2025-12-04_12-43-49.zip)

summary: [GDScript] Script circular references break cache

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in 4.6 dev since b4203f7f643640dd0a0212f454e8894aac018021

not reproducible without that commit

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (78d91947f) - macOS Sequoia (15.7.1) - Multi-window, 2 monitors - Metal (Mobile) - integrated Apple M1 (Apple7) - Apple M1 (8 threads) - 8.00 GiB memory

### Issue description

Scripts which depend on each other seemingly break the cache, resulting in becoming unsaved buffers when anything opens them and embedding themselves in scenes if a scene opened it, generally breaking the project as soon as you press save. It's sufficient to just open the scripts in the editor.

seems to be a side effect of b4203f7f643640dd0a0212f454e8894aac018021 and is unaffected by #113580

### Steps to reproduce

- Make two scripts with global class names
- Make a var referring to the other one
- Open the scripts, one won't work.

These will do the job:

```gdscript
# a.gd
extends Node
class_name CircularRefA
@export var other: CircularRefB
```

```gdscript
# b.gd
extends Node
class_name CircularRefB
@export var other: CircularRefA
```


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

MRP: [circular-ref-cache.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23943189/circular-ref-cache.zip)

steps:
- open `a.gd` (or `b.gd`, if that one works for some reason)

it should show up as an unsaved buffer in the script editor and complain about hiding a global script class.

summary: Adding font fallbacks in Import Defaults makes Godot unable to run embedded game window and corrupts project.godot

description.text: ### Tested versions Reproducible in: - 4.5.1.stable.official - 4.6.dev5 - 4.4.1.stable ### System information Windows 11 - Godot v4.5.1.stable.official - Vulkan (Forward+) - AMD Ryzen 7 9700X 8-Core Processor - Dedicated GPU NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5070 Ti ### Issue description Adding Font Fallbacks Array in Project Settings -> Import Defaults will generate at error in the console: ` ERROR: scene/main/window.cpp:1050 - Attempting to make child window exclusive, but the parent window already has another exclusive child. This window: /root/@EditorNode@20438/@Panel@14/@EditorQuickOpenDialog@20362, parent window: /root, current exclusive child window: /root/@EditorNode@20438/@Panel@14/@ProjectSettingsEditor@2557` Attempting to run the project with font fallbacks in Import Defaults settings will open the Godot project manager screen (Godot starting screen whenever you run the Godot Engine .exe), as seen below Godot will also automatically switch the renderer to Compatibility Mode (OpenGL) and prints: `Embedded windows can't have a minimum size.` Closing Godot in this state and reopening will make the project marked by Godot as "Missing Project" and gives error "Unknown version" The project.godot file will not be able to be reimported into Godot Engine. If before quitting the project, all font fallbacks are removed from Import Defaults settings, the project will work as normal. ### Steps to reproduce 1. Make new project 2. Project Settings -> Import Defaults -> Importer: Font Data (Dynamic Fonts) 3. Fallbacks settings: Add element in Fallbacks Array. As soon as you click the quick load icon, the `ERROR: scene/main/window.cpp:1050` will print in the console. 4. Setting a valid font as fallback will reproduce the other errors. Godot will be unable to run the game and closing Godot will mark the project as "Missing project" on the Godot starting screen. ### Minimal reproduction project (MRP) N/A. Reproducible in brand new project in Godot v4.5.1.stable.official

summary: Fix transient docks not saving floating positions

description.text:
Fixes #113259

Previously, docks that were closed from the floating position didn't remember to open back in their floating position. This PR changes it so any floating dock closed from code (not through clicking the window close), will remember its position on the screen and re-open in that position.

summary: Audio crackling/clipping when running the Game while in a Discord voice call

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in v.4.6.dev5, v.4.5.1.stable, v.3.6.2.stable, v.3.4.stable

### System information

Windows 10 - Windows 11 - Godot v4.5.1.stable - Vulkan (Mobile)

### Issue description

When running the game and playing an audio stream(most noticeable for music tracks) it sounds well but joining a voice call in a Discord server makes the sound start crackling/clipping and it does so with a few seconds to half a minute interval until leaving the voice call, then the music sounds well again. Rejoining the voice call make the issue reappear.

I tested this on two different PCs and two other tried it on their PC with the same problem. I tested with different audio files. I tested both .wav and .mp3. I tested with different bit depth, even 8-bit. I ensured it was 2-channel audio. I tried adjusting the latency. I tried with mix rate 44100 Hz and 48000 Hz. Non of these had any effect on the issue.


My guess is that this has to do with thread priority being too low for the audio thread.
To investigate this I built the engine from source(v.4.5.1.stable) and added the following to 
`void AudioDriverWASAPI::thread_func(void *p_udata)` in audio_driver_wasapi.cpp :


```
void AudioDriverWASAPI::thread_func(void *p_udata) {
	CoInitializeEx(nullptr, COINIT_APARTMENTTHREADED);


	// Try associating the audio thread with "Pro Audio" task to prevent audio clipping issues https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/avrt/nf-avrt-avsetmmthreadcharacteristicsw
	DWORD audio_task_index = 0;
	AvSetMmThreadCharacteristics(TEXT("Pro Audio"), &audio_task_index);
```
This fixes the issue for me but I'm not sure about a valid solution to this.
I also tried with just setting the thread priority to _THREAD_PRIORITY_HIGHEST_ but that doesn't fix the issue, but associating the thread with the "Pro Audio" task does fix it.


### Steps to reproduce

- play a scene and listen, music plays and all is well.
- join a discord voice call in a server and the music starts crackling/clipping on a few seconds up to half a minute interval
- leaving voice call makes the issue go away until joining again.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

Project setup: 
- new project
- using WASAPI driver
- new scene
- add AudioStreamPlayer and set autoplay. Specify a music stream or a longer audio clip, preferably looping.

summary: Hide or show 3D Joints when visibility is toggled on that node or any parent node.
Rework `Camera3D` preview

description.text:
Bugfix:
- Fix https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101716 (see solution suggested in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101716#issuecomment-2600771589).
- Most likely also fix https://github.com/godotengine/godot/issues/101755 (but I can not reproduce it in current master).

Enhancement:
- Addresses a problem raised in https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/90778#issuecomment-2849314171 (there is no way to collapse preview).

---

### Implementation details

#### 1. Visual glitches during selection and switching

Instead of reusing `TexturePreview` and getting `SubViewport` texture, `Camera3DPreview` is now not inherited from `TexturePreview` and uses the following structure: `AspectRatioContainer` -> `SubViewportContainer` -> `SubViewport`. 

#### 2. Collapsing preview

Now preview uses `FoldableContainer` and can be collapsed.
Folding state is stored the same way as preview grid state in `StyleBoxPreview` - using static variable. This means that all `Camera3DPreview` in editor will have the same folding state. This state is not saved/loaded on editor start/quit, therefore it is saved only during the current session. 
By default preview is not folded.
The only thing I'm not sure about is the `FoldableContainer` theme - should I create a new style variation to make it visually more appropriate, or leave everything as it is.  Most likely new style variation will be better, I'll try to make later.

---

summary: X11 Input Delay Fix

description.text:
This PR lowers the timeout in `DisplayServerX11::_wait_for_events()` from 1 second to 1,000 microseconds in order to fix the issue of some input events being delayed, causing a noticeable stutter in relative mouse motion for example.

Timeout value of 1s:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7c181edc-7ffa-4fec-afd5-7a8ff770ada5

Timeout value of 1,000us:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/00d824bd-cdac-4d9c-bd3b-b8b71aa6cfbe

This issue was not present in 4.5-stable, but was re-introduced in 4.5.1-stable as a side-effect of fixing a rare crash by removing a call to `_check_pending_events()` on the main thread during `DisplayServerX11::process_events()`.

This should solve the stutter up to 240hz (Though I've only been able to test 60hz, 144hz, and 165hz), and did not have a noticeable impact on CPU usage % on either of my machines in both the editor and running projects.

Fixes #113188

summary: Editor thumbnail generation prints a transparent viewport warning if a material uses subsurface scattering

description.text:
### Tested versions

- Reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable, 4.6.dev2, 4.6.dev5
- Not reproducible in: 4.5.stable

### System information

Godot v4.6.dev (fbc953976) - Fedora Linux 43 (KDE Plasma Desktop Edition) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 1 monitor - Vulkan (Forward+) - dedicated NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5090 (nvidia; 580.95.05) - AMD Ryzen 9 9950X3D 16-Core Processor (32 threads) - 62.33 GiB memory

### Issue description

Following the merge of https://github.com/godotengine/godot/pull/108206, this warning is printed when a material uses SSS due to its thumbnail generation (thumbnails use a transparent viewport):

```
WARNING: Sub-surface scattering is not supported in viewports with a transparent background. Disabling SSS in transparent viewport.
     at: _render_scene (servers/rendering/renderer_rd/forward_clustered/render_forward_clustered.cpp:1931)
```

We should find a way to silence this specific warning during thumbnail generation. Maybe ignoring the warning when `Engine::is_editor_hint()` is `true` would suffice?

Alternatively, we could switch to a chroma keying approach for thumbnail generation, so that we don't need to use a transparent viewport. However, this is much more involved for a small visual change (the impact of SSS on thumbnails is not that significant). We'd also need to keep the transparent viewport codepath around for transparent materials, as this approach is only viable for opaque materials.

### Steps to reproduce

- Create a StandardMaterial3D and enable subsurface scattering on it.
- Save the BaseMaterial3D to a `.tres` file.
- Close the editor.
- Remove the [editor's cache folder](https://docs.godotengine.org/en/stable/tutorials/io/data_paths.html#editor-data-paths) to force the thumbnail to regenerate.
- Reopen the editor. Look at the Output panel showing a warning.

### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[test_sss_thumbnail.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23918858/test_sss_thumbnail.zip)

summary: Accessibility: Re-apply stored name when recreating nodes

description.text:
## Summary
- Fix tab names not being announced by screen readers in the Godot editor
- When `_ensure_node()` recreates an AccessKit node, re-apply the stored name so it persists

## Problem
When TabContainer calls `accessibility_update_add_related_controls()`, it triggers `_ensure_node()` which creates a new empty node. TabBar only sets tab properties when `accessibility_item_dirty` is true, so recreated nodes were pushed without names, causing screen readers to announce "page tab" without the actual tab name (Scene, Inspector, etc.).

## Solution
In `_ensure_node()`, after creating a new node, check if a name was previously stored and re-apply it. This ensures tab names persist across node recreations.

## Test plan
- [x] Build with accessibility enabled
- [x] Open Godot editor with screen reader
- [x] Navigate to tabs (Scene, Import, FileSystem, etc.)
- [x] Verify screen reader announces tab names, not just "page tab"

### System information

linux

### Issue description

I haven’t tested this further, but I noticed that crashes occur after changing a 3D node (such as a CSG or MeshInstance) to a Control node. Regular 3D nodes do nothing when their type is changed. I’m not sure what might be causing this, but it seems like the editor simply can’t render it.

### Steps to reproduce

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1e2f0269-8aca-496a-b69c-a9fe7f519bcd

I also noticed that this doesn’t always happen. In the last example in the video, you can see that the error doesn’t occur, and it appeared only after saving the scene. Maybe it’s not the saving itself, but the fact that the scene was updated and became active for this error to appear.

I tested this in my own project, but it also happens in an empty one. You just need to take a CSG or MeshInstance and convert it into a Control node, or do the same with its parent.

summary: History navigation is broken after navigating forward

description.text:
### Tested versions

Reproducible in: 4.5.1.stable, 4.6.dev5



### System information

Godot v4.6.dev5 - Linux Mint 22.2 (Zara) on X11 - X11 display driver, Multi-window, 2 monitors - OpenGL 3 (Compatibility) - NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 (nvidia; 570.195.03) - Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-4790K CPU @ 4.00GHz (8 threads) - 15.48 GiB memory

### Issue description

I often `ctrl + left click` class name or a method to quickly check something out in the file where the class is defined. Then I want go to back where I was using `alt + left arrow` (History Previous).

I would expect to always end up where I was just before `ctrl + left clicking`. Most of the times I do. But very often I end up in a completely different file instead and then I have to manually find my way back to where I was before. As if the file was not added to history for some reason.

It seems to be triggered by navigating forward.


### Steps to reproduce

See the attached MRP.

1. Open files `main.gd` and `other.gd`.
2. Reload the project while `main.gd` is open.
3. After the project reloads, `main.gd` should be open. Go to file `other.gd` by clicking on it in the file list.
4. Navigate back to `main.gd` using history.
5. Navigate forward to `other.gd` using history.
6. Navigate back to `main.gd` using history.
7. Go to `other.gd` by clicking on it in the file list, not by using history.
8. From now on nothing is added to the history.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/aaa91f1a-177a-4fbf-b0ce-6937c6365f28)


### Minimal reproduction project (MRP)

[history_issue.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/23904946/history_issue.zip)
